title: strobilanthes repanda
intro: Strobilanthes repanda, also known as the 'Khas', is a plant species that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Tibetan and Nepalese cultures, for its medicinal properties. The leaves of Strobilanthes repanda contain flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and improving digestion. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in various herbal preparations, including capsules and tinctures.
